papers.title: LongCat-Flash-Prover: Advancing Native Formal Reasoning via Agentic Tool-Integrated Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract A 560-billion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model advances formal reasoning in Lean4 through tool-integrated reasoning with a hybrid framework and hierarchical policy optimization for stable training on long-horizon tasks.  AI-generated summary We introduce LongCat-Flash-Prover, a flagship 560-billion-parameter open-source Mixture-of- Experts (MoE) model that advances Native Formal Reasoning in Lean4 through agentic tool-integrated reasoning (TIR). We decompose the native formal reasoning task into three independent formal capabilities, i.e., auto-formalization, sketching, and proving. To facilitate these capabilities, we propose a Hybrid-Experts Iteration Framework to expand high-quality task trajectories, including generating a formal statement based on a given informal problem, producing a whole-proof directly from the statement, or a lemma-style sketch. During agentic RL, we present a Hierarchical Importance Sampling Policy Optimization (HisPO) algorithm, which aims to stabilize the MoE model training on such long-horizon tasks. It employs a gradient masking strategy that accounts for the policy staleness and the inherent train-inference engine discrepancies at both sequence and token levels. Additionally, we also incorporate theorem consistency and legality detection mechanisms to eliminate reward hacking issues. Extensive evaluations show that our LongCat-Flash-Prover sets a new state-of-the-art for open-weights models in both auto-formalization and theorem proving. Demonstrating remarkable sample efficiency, it achieves a 97.1% pass rate on MiniF2F-Test using only 72 inference budget per problem. On more challenging benchmarks, it solves 70.8% of ProverBench and 41.5% of PutnamBench with no more than 220 attempts per problem, significantly outperforming existing open-weights baselines.

ru.title: Огромная смесь экспертов для автоматического доказательства математических теорем
ru.desc: Представлена модель LongCat-Flash-Prover на архитектуре Mixture-of-Experts с 560 миллиардами параметров для формального доказательства теорем в Lean4. Модель использует интегрированное инструментальное рассуждение, разлагая задачу на три независимые компоненты: автоматизацию формализации, создание эскизов доказательств и само доказательство. Для стабильной подготовки на длинных последовательностях действий предложен алгоритм HisPO с иерархической выборкой и маскированием градиентов на уровне токенов и последовательностей. Модель достигает рекордных результатов среди открытых моделей на бенчмарках формального доказательства, решая 97.1% задач MiniF2F-Test с минимальным числом попыток.

en.title: Revolutionizing Formal Reasoning with LongCat-Flash-Prover
en.desc: The paper presents LongCat-Flash-Prover, a large Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model with 560 billion parameters designed to enhance formal reasoning in Lean4. It breaks down the reasoning task into three key components: auto-formalization, sketching, and proving, using a Hybrid-Experts Iteration Framework to improve task performance. The training process employs a Hierarchical Importance Sampling Policy Optimization (HisPO) algorithm to ensure stability during long-horizon tasks, addressing issues like policy staleness. The model demonstrates exceptional efficiency, achieving high success rates on various benchmarks while minimizing attempts needed for problem-solving.

zh.title: 长猫闪证人：形式推理的新突破
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种名为LongCat-Flash-Prover的5600亿参数的混合专家模型，旨在通过工具集成推理（TIR）来提升Lean4中的形式推理能力。我们将形式推理任务分解为三个独立的能力：自动形式化、草图绘制和证明。为支持这些能力，我们提出了一种混合专家迭代框架，以扩展高质量的任务轨迹，并在长时间任务上稳定训练模型。通过引入分层重要性采样策略优化算法（HisPO），我们有效解决了训练过程中的政策过时和推理引擎差异问题，最终在多个基准测试中取得了优异的表现。

papers.title: Speed by Simplicity: A Single-Stream Architecture for Fast Audio-Video Generative Foundation Model
papers.abstract: Abstract daVinci-MagiHuman is an open-source audio-video generative model that synchronizes text, video, and audio through a single-stream Transformer architecture, achieving high-quality human-centric content generation with efficient inference capabilities.  AI-generated summary We present daVinci-MagiHuman, an open-source audio-video generative foundation model for human-centric generation. daVinci-MagiHuman jointly generates synchronized video and audio using a single-stream Transformer that processes text, video, and audio within a unified token sequence via self-attention only. This single-stream design avoids the complexity of multi-stream or cross-attention architectures while remaining easy to optimize with standard training and inference infrastructure. The model is particularly strong in human-centric scenarios, producing expressive facial performance, natural speech-expression coordination, realistic body motion, and precise audio-video synchronization. It supports multilingual spoken generation across Chinese (Mandarin and Cantonese), English, Japanese, Korean, German, and French. For efficient inference, we combine the single-stream backbone with model distillation, latent-space super-resolution, and a Turbo VAE decoder, enabling generation of a 5-second 256p video in 2 seconds on a single H100 GPU. In automatic evaluation, daVinci-MagiHuman achieves the highest visual quality and text alignment among leading open models, along with the lowest word error rate (14.60%) for speech intelligibility. In pairwise human evaluation, it achieves win rates of 80.0% against Ovi 1.1 and 60.9% against LTX 2.3 over 2000 comparisons. We open-source the complete model stack, including the base model, the distilled model, the super-resolution model, and the inference codebase.

ru.title: Единопоточный синтез синхронизированного видео и аудио человека из текста
ru.desc: daVinci-MagiHuman — это открытая мультимодальная генеративная модель, которая синтезирует видео и аудио на основе текстового описания, используя единую архитектуру Transformer без кросс-внимания. Модель обрабатывает текст, видео и аудио в едином потоке токенов через механизм самовнимания, что упрощает оптимизацию и инференс. Система специализирована на создании контента с людьми, генерируя выразительную мимику, синхронизированную речь и естественную динамику движений, поддерживая множество языков. Благодаря дистилляции модели и оптимизированному декодеру, инференс занимает всего 2 секунды для генерации 5-секундного видео на одном GPU H100.

en.title: Revolutionizing Human-Centric Content Generation with daVinci-MagiHuman
en.desc: daVinci-MagiHuman is an innovative open-source model designed for generating synchronized audio and video content, focusing on human-centric applications. It utilizes a single-stream Transformer architecture that processes text, video, and audio in a unified manner, simplifying the generation process while maintaining high quality. The model excels in producing realistic human expressions and speech, achieving impressive synchronization between audio and video. With efficient inference techniques, it allows for rapid generation of content across multiple languages, setting new benchmarks in visual quality and speech intelligibility.

zh.title: 高效生成人类中心内容的音视频模型
zh.desc: daVinci-MagiHuman是一个开源的音视频生成模型，能够通过单流Transformer架构同步文本、视频和音频，生成高质量的人类中心内容。该模型使用自注意力机制在统一的token序列中处理文本、视频和音频，避免了多流或交叉注意力架构的复杂性。它在生成自然的面部表情、语音表达协调、逼真的身体动作和精确的音视频同步方面表现出色，并支持多种语言的生成。通过模型蒸馏和潜在空间超分辨率等技术，daVinci-MagiHuman在单个H100 GPU上能够高效生成5秒的256p视频。

papers.title: VideoDetective: Clue Hunting via both Extrinsic Query and Intrinsic Relevance for Long Video Understanding
papers.abstract: Abstract VideoDetective framework improves long video understanding by integrating query-to-segment relevance and inter-segment affinity through visual-temporal graphs and hypothesis verification loops.  AI-generated summary Long video understanding remains challenging for multimodal large language models (MLLMs) due to limited context windows, which necessitate identifying sparse query-relevant video segments. However, existing methods predominantly localize clues based solely on the query, overlooking the video's intrinsic structure and varying relevance across segments. To address this, we propose VideoDetective, a framework that integrates query-to-segment relevance and inter-segment affinity for effective clue hunting in long-video question answering. Specifically, we divide a video into various segments and represent them as a visual-temporal affinity graph built from visual similarity and temporal proximity. We then perform a Hypothesis-Verification-Refinement loop to estimate relevance scores of observed segments to the query and propagate them to unseen segments, yielding a global relevance distribution that guides the localization of the most critical segments for final answering with sparse observation. Experiments show our method consistently achieves substantial gains across a wide range of mainstream MLLMs on representative benchmarks, with accuracy improvements of up to 7.5% on VideoMME-long. Our code is available at https://videodetective.github.io/
affiliations: Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Детективный поиск ключевых фрагментов в длинных видео через граф визуально-временной связанности
ru.desc: Статья представляет фреймворк VideoDetective, который улучшает понимание длинных видеороликов мультимодальными большими языковыми моделями путём интеграции релевантности запроса к сегментам видео и связанности между сегментами. Метод разделяет видео на сегменты и строит граф визуально-временной близости на основе визуального сходства и временной соседства сегментов. Фреймворк использует цикл гипотеза-верификация-уточнение для оценки релевантности сегментов к запросу и распространения этих оценок на невидимые сегменты, получая глобальное распределение релевантности. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что метод дает улучшения точности до 7,5% на бенчмарке VideoMME-long для различных мультимодальных LLM.

en.title: Unlocking Long Video Insights with VideoDetective
en.desc: The VideoDetective framework enhances the understanding of long videos by using visual-temporal graphs to connect relevant segments to queries and assess their relationships. It addresses the limitations of existing methods that focus only on query-based localization, which often ignores the video's overall structure. By segmenting the video and applying a Hypothesis-Verification-Refinement loop, the framework calculates relevance scores for segments, allowing it to identify the most important parts for answering questions. This approach has shown significant improvements in accuracy for multimodal large language models on various benchmarks.

zh.title: 视频理解的新突破：VideoDetective框架
zh.desc: VideoDetective框架通过视觉-时间图和假设验证循环，提升了长视频理解的能力。该方法解决了多模态大语言模型在处理长视频时的上下文窗口限制问题，能够有效识别与查询相关的视频片段。我们将视频划分为多个片段，并构建视觉相似性和时间接近性的图，以表示片段之间的关系。实验结果表明，该方法在多个主流基准测试中显著提高了准确率，最高可达7.5%。

papers.title: mSFT: Addressing Dataset Mixtures Overfiting Heterogeneously in Multi-task SFT
papers.abstract: Abstract Multi-task supervised fine-tuning with heterogeneous learning dynamics benefits from an iterative overfitting-aware search algorithm that improves performance across diverse datasets and compute budgets.  AI-generated summary Current language model training commonly applies multi-task Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) using a homogeneous compute budget across all sub-datasets. This approach is fundamentally sub-optimal: heterogeneous learning dynamics cause faster-learning tasks to overfit early while slower ones remain under-fitted. To address this, we introduce mSFT, an iterative, overfitting-aware search algorithm for multi-task data mixtures. mSFT trains the model on an active mixture, identifies and excludes the earliest overfitting sub-dataset, and reverts to that specific optimal checkpoint before continuing. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that mSFT consistently outperforms 4 baselines across 10 benchmarks and 6 base models. Further analysis confirms mSFT maintains robust gains across diverse dataset sizes, task granularities, and is insensitive to its single new hyperparameter (compute budget). Notably, at low compute budget, mSFT can improve performance while lowering training FLOPs. Ultimately, mSFT establishes a practical overfitting-aware algorithm for multi-task SFT that maximizes the potential of models across diverse data mixtures.

ru.title: Интеллектуальная смесь данных: предотвращение переобучения в мультизадачной доточке
ru.desc: Статья представляет алгоритм mSFT для оптимизации мультизадачной доточки языковых моделей. Проблема заключается в том, что разные задачи имеют разную скорость обучения, что приводит к переобучению быстрых задач и недообучению медленных при одинаковом бюджете вычислений. Предложенный итеративный алгоритм отслеживает переобучение каждого поддатасета, исключает его из смеси данных и возвращается к оптимальной контрольной точке. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что mSFT превосходит базовые методы на множестве бенчмарков и моделей, особенно при ограниченном вычислительном бюджете.

en.title: Maximizing Multi-Task Learning with mSFT: An Overfitting-Aware Approach
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach called mSFT for multi-task supervised fine-tuning (SFT) that addresses the issue of heterogeneous learning dynamics in training. Traditional SFT methods use a uniform compute budget, which can lead to faster-learning tasks overfitting while slower tasks remain under-fitted. The mSFT algorithm iteratively identifies and removes overfitting sub-datasets, allowing the model to revert to optimal checkpoints and continue training effectively. Evaluations show that mSFT outperforms existing methods across various benchmarks, demonstrating its ability to enhance model performance while being efficient with compute resources.

zh.title: mSFT：优化多任务微调的过拟合算法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为mSFT的多任务监督微调算法，旨在解决异构学习动态带来的过拟合问题。传统的多任务微调方法在所有子数据集上使用相同的计算预算，导致快速学习的任务过早过拟合，而慢速学习的任务则未能充分拟合。mSFT通过迭代的方式，主动识别并排除最早过拟合的子数据集，并在继续训练之前恢复到最佳检查点。实验结果表明，mSFT在多个基准测试中表现优于四个基线模型，并在不同数据集规模和任务细粒度下保持稳健的性能提升。

papers.title: On the Direction of RLVR Updates for LLM Reasoning: Identification and Exploitation
papers.abstract: Abstract Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards improves language model reasoning by focusing on the direction of parameter updates rather than their magnitude, enabling better test-time extrapolation and training-time reweighting methods.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has substantially improved the reasoning capabilities of large language models. While existing analyses identify that RLVR-induced changes are sparse, they primarily focus on the magnitude of these updates, largely overlooking their direction. In this work, we argue that the direction of updates is a more critical lens for understanding RLVR's effects, which can be captured by the signed, token-level log probability difference Δlog p between the base and final RLVR models. Through statistical analysis and token-replacement interventions, we demonstrate that Δlog p more effectively identifies sparse, yet reasoning-critical updates than magnitude-based metrics (\eg divergence or entropy). Building on this insight, we propose two practical applications: (1) a test-time extrapolation method that amplifies the policy along the learned Δlog p direction to improve reasoning accuracy without further training; (2) a training-time reweighting method that focuses learning on low-probability (corresponding to higher Δlog p) tokens, which improves reasoning performance across models and benchmarks. Our work establishes the direction of change as a key principle for analyzing and improving RLVR.

ru.title: Направление изменений важнее их величины
ru.desc: В статье исследуется обучение с подкреплением и проверяемыми наградами (RLVR) для улучшения способностей больших языковых моделей к рассуждению. Авторы показывают, что направление изменений параметров модели более важно, чем их величина, и предлагают использовать знаковую разницу логарифмических вероятностей для анализа обновлений. На основе этого понимания они разработали два метода: тестовый метод экстраполяции, который усиливает политику без переобучения, и метод переваживания на этапе обучения, сосредоточивающийся на маловероятных токенах. Работа демонстрирует, что фокус на направлении изменений, а не на их амплитуде, является ключевым принципом для анализа и улучшения RLVR.

en.title: Unlocking Language Model Reasoning Through Directional Updates
en.desc: This paper discusses how reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) enhances the reasoning abilities of language models by emphasizing the direction of parameter updates instead of just their size. The authors highlight that understanding the direction of updates, represented by the signed log probability difference, is crucial for identifying important changes that improve reasoning. They present two applications: one that enhances reasoning accuracy during testing by following the learned update direction, and another that adjusts training to focus on less probable tokens, leading to better performance. Overall, the study positions the direction of updates as a vital factor in analyzing and optimizing RLVR.

zh.title: 更新方向：提升推理能力的关键
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）如何提升大型语言模型的推理能力。我们发现，更新的方向比更新的幅度更能有效地反映RLVR的影响。通过统计分析和标记替换干预，我们证明了更新方向的变化（Δlog p）能够更好地识别出稀疏但关键的推理更新。基于这一发现，我们提出了两种实际应用：一种是在测试时沿着Δlog p方向增强策略的方法，另一种是在训练时重加权低概率标记的方法，以提高推理性能。

papers.title: WorldCache: Content-Aware Caching for Accelerated Video World Models
papers.abstract: Abstract WorldCache improves diffusion transformer inference by adaptively reusing features through motion-adaptive thresholds and saliency-weighted drift estimation, achieving faster processing with minimal quality loss.  AI-generated summary Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) power high-fidelity video world models but remain computationally expensive due to sequential denoising and costly spatio-temporal attention. Training-free feature caching accelerates inference by reusing intermediate activations across denoising steps; however, existing methods largely rely on a Zero-Order Hold assumption i.e., reusing cached features as static snapshots when global drift is small. This often leads to ghosting artifacts, blur, and motion inconsistencies in dynamic scenes. We propose WorldCache, a Perception-Constrained Dynamical Caching framework that improves both when and how to reuse features. WorldCache introduces motion-adaptive thresholds, saliency-weighted drift estimation, optimal approximation via blending and warping, and phase-aware threshold scheduling across diffusion steps. Our cohesive approach enables adaptive, motion-consistent feature reuse without retraining. On Cosmos-Predict2.5-2B evaluated on PAI-Bench, WorldCache achieves 2.3times inference speedup while preserving 99.4\% of baseline quality, substantially outperforming prior training-free caching approaches. Our code can be accessed on https://umair1221.github.io/World-Cache/{World-Cache}.
affiliations: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence, UAE

ru.title: Умное кэширование признаков для быстрого вывода видеотрансформеров
ru.desc: WorldCache — это фреймворк для адаптивного переиспользования признаков в диффузионных трансформерах, который применяет пороги, чувствительные к движению, и взвешенную по значимости оценку дрейфа. Метод позволяет ускорить инференс видеомоделей в 2.3 раза без переобучения, сохраняя 99.4% качества исходной модели. Основная инновация состоит в преодолении ограничений нулевого порядка удержания через оптимальное смешивание и деформацию признаков на разных этапах диффузии. Подход обеспечивает последовательное переиспользование промежуточных активаций при сохранении согласованности движения и устранении артефактов размытия в динамичных сценах.

en.title: Accelerating Video Inference with Smart Feature Reuse
en.desc: WorldCache is a novel framework designed to enhance the efficiency of diffusion transformers during video inference. It achieves this by adaptively reusing features through techniques like motion-adaptive thresholds and saliency-weighted drift estimation, which help maintain quality while speeding up processing. Unlike previous methods that treat cached features as static, WorldCache dynamically adjusts feature reuse based on motion and scene changes, reducing artifacts like ghosting and blur. The results show a significant improvement, with a 2.3 times speedup in inference while retaining 99.4% of the original quality, making it a powerful tool for real-time video applications.

zh.title: 动态特征重用，提升推理速度！
zh.desc: WorldCache 是一种改进扩散变换器推理的方法，通过运动自适应阈值和显著性加权漂移估计，动态地重用特征，从而实现更快的处理速度和最小的质量损失。该方法解决了现有特征缓存技术在动态场景中常见的鬼影、模糊和运动不一致问题。WorldCache 采用了感知约束的动态缓存框架，优化了特征重用的时机和方式。通过在扩散步骤中引入混合、变形和相位感知阈值调度，WorldCache 在保持高质量的同时，实现了推理速度的显著提升。

papers.title: PivotRL: High Accuracy Agentic Post-Training at Low Compute Cost
papers.abstract: Abstract PivotRL is a novel framework that combines supervised fine-tuning efficiency with reinforcement learning generalization by using local rollouts and functional-equivalent action rewards to achieve better performance with reduced compute costs.  AI-generated summary Post-training for long-horizon agentic tasks has a tension between compute efficiency and generalization. While supervised fine-tuning (SFT) is compute efficient, it often suffers from out-of-domain (OOD) degradation. Conversely, end-to-end reinforcement learning (E2E RL) preserves OOD capabilities, but incurs high compute costs due to many turns of on-policy rollout. We introduce PivotRL, a novel framework that operates on existing SFT trajectories to combine the compute efficiency of SFT with the OOD accuracy of E2E RL. PivotRL relies on two key mechanisms: first, it executes local, on-policy rollouts and filters for pivots: informative intermediate turns where sampled actions exhibit high variance in outcomes; second, it utilizes rewards for functional-equivalent actions rather than demanding strict string matching with the SFT data demonstration. We theoretically show that these mechanisms incentivize strong learning signals with high natural gradient norm, while maximally preserving policy probability ordering on actions unrelated to training tasks. In comparison to standard SFT on identical data, we demonstrate that PivotRL achieves +4.17% higher in-domain accuracy on average across four agentic domains, and +10.04% higher OOD accuracy in non-agentic tasks. Notably, on agentic coding tasks, PivotRL achieves competitive accuracy with E2E RL with 4x fewer rollout turns. PivotRL is adopted by NVIDIA's Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B, acting as the workhorse in production-scale agentic post-training.

ru.title: Эффективное обучение агентов: SFT и RL в одном фреймворке
ru.desc: PivotRL — это фреймворк, который комбинирует эффективность supervised fine-tuning с обобщающей способностью reinforcement learning через локальные rollouts и награды за функционально эквивалентные действия. Метод решает проблему баланса между вычислительной эффективностью и устойчивостью к распределению вне обучающего множества (OOD) при постобучении агентных моделей. Ключевые механизмы включают выявление информативных промежуточных точек (pivots), где действия имеют высокую дисперсию исходов, и использование функциональной эквивалентности вместо точного совпадения с демонстрациями. Экспериментально показано, что PivotRL достигает лучшей точности как внутри распределения (+4.17%), так и вне его (+10.04%), при этом требуя в 4 раза меньше rollout-ов по сравнению с полным reinforcement learning.

en.title: PivotRL: Bridging Efficiency and Generalization in AI Training
en.desc: PivotRL is a new framework that enhances the efficiency of supervised fine-tuning (SFT) while maintaining the generalization capabilities of reinforcement learning (RL). It achieves this by using local rollouts to identify key decision points, or pivots, where actions lead to varied outcomes. Additionally, it employs functional-equivalent action rewards, allowing for more flexible learning without strict adherence to SFT data. As a result, PivotRL shows improved accuracy in both in-domain and out-of-domain tasks, significantly reducing the computational cost compared to traditional end-to-end RL methods.

zh.title: PivotRL：高效与泛化的完美结合
zh.desc: PivotRL是一种新颖的框架，它结合了监督微调的高效性和强化学习的泛化能力。通过使用局部回合和功能等效的动作奖励，PivotRL在计算成本降低的同时实现了更好的性能。该框架通过执行局部的在线回合并过滤出重要的中间步骤，来增强学习信号。与标准的监督微调相比，PivotRL在多个任务上表现出更高的准确性，尤其是在长时间的代理任务中。

papers.title: MemDLM: Memory-Enhanced DLM Training
papers.abstract: Abstract MemDLM addresses the train-inference mismatch in diffusion language models by incorporating a bi-level optimization framework with parametric memory that enhances both training efficiency and inference performance through adaptive denoising processes.  AI-generated summary Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) offer attractive advantages over Auto-Regressive (AR) models, such as full-attention parallel decoding and flexible generation. However, they suffer from a notable train-inference mismatch: DLMs are trained with a static, single-step masked prediction objective, but deployed through a multi-step progressive denoising trajectory. We propose MemDLM (Memory-Enhanced DLM), which narrows this gap by embedding a simulated denoising process into training via Bi-level Optimization. An inner loop updates a set of fast weights, forming a Parametric Memory that captures the local trajectory experience of each sample, while an outer loop updates the base model conditioned on this memory. By offloading memorization pressure from token representations to parameters, MemDLM yields faster convergence and lower training loss. Moreover, the inner loop can be re-enabled at inference time as an adaptation step, yielding additional gains on long-context understanding. We find that, when activated at inference time, this Parametric Memory acts as an emergent in-weight retrieval mechanism, helping MemDLM further reduce token-level attention bottlenecks on challenging Needle-in-a-Haystack retrieval tasks. Code: https://github.com/JarvisPei/MemDLM.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Память в весах: мост между обучением и инференсом диффузионных моделей
ru.desc: MemDLM решает проблему несоответствия между обучением и инференсом в диффузионных языковых моделях, используя двухуровневую оптимизацию с параметрической памятью. Внутренний цикл обновляет быстрые веса, которые захватывают траекторию шумоподавления для каждого образца, а внешний цикл обновляет основную модель на основе этой памяти. Такой подход переносит нагрузку запоминания с представлений токенов на параметры модели, что ускоряет сходимость и снижает потери при обучении. На этапе инференса параметрическая память работает как механизм поиска в весах, улучшая обработку длинных контекстов и справляясь с задачами поиска информации.

en.title: Enhancing Diffusion Language Models with Memory for Better Training and Inference
en.desc: MemDLM introduces a bi-level optimization framework to improve the training and inference processes of diffusion language models (DLMs). It addresses the train-inference mismatch by using a parametric memory that captures the denoising trajectory during training. This approach allows for faster convergence and lower training loss by offloading memorization from token representations to model parameters. Additionally, the parametric memory can be utilized during inference to enhance long-context understanding and reduce attention bottlenecks in complex retrieval tasks.

zh.title: 记忆增强，提升推理效率
zh.desc: MemDLM（记忆增强扩散语言模型）通过引入双层优化框架和参数记忆，解决了扩散语言模型在训练和推理之间的不匹配问题。该模型在训练过程中嵌入了模拟去噪过程，从而提高了训练效率和推理性能。内层循环更新快速权重，形成参数记忆，捕捉每个样本的局部轨迹经验，而外层循环则基于此记忆更新基础模型。通过将记忆压力从标记表示转移到参数，MemDLM实现了更快的收敛和更低的训练损失，同时在推理时重新启用内层循环可以进一步提升长上下文理解能力。

papers.title: AnimalCLAP: Taxonomy-Aware Language-Audio Pretraining for Species Recognition and Trait Inference
papers.abstract: Abstract AnimalCLAP is a taxonomy-aware language-audio framework that uses hierarchical biological information to improve species classification from vocalizations, achieving better performance than CLAP by leveraging ecological traits and taxonomic structures.  AI-generated summary Animal vocalizations provide crucial insights for wildlife assessment, particularly in complex environments such as forests, aiding species identification and ecological monitoring. Recent advances in deep learning have enabled automatic species classification from their vocalizations. However, classifying species unseen during training remains challenging. To address this limitation, we introduce AnimalCLAP, a taxonomy-aware language-audio framework comprising a new dataset and model that incorporate hierarchical biological information. Specifically, our vocalization dataset consists of 4,225 hours of recordings covering 6,823 species, annotated with 22 ecological traits. The AnimalCLAP model is trained on this dataset to align audio and textual representations using taxonomic structures, improving the recognition of unseen species. We demonstrate that our proposed model effectively infers ecological and biological attributes of species directly from their vocalizations, achieving superior performance compared to CLAP. Our dataset, code, and models will be publicly available at https://dahlian00.github.io/AnimalCLAP_Page/.

ru.title: Таксономия в звуках: экология видов через аудио
ru.desc: AnimalCLAP — это таксономически-ориентированная框架для совместного обучения на аудио и текстовых данных, которая использует иерархическую биологическую информацию для улучшения классификации видов по их вокализациям. Модель обучается на большом датасете из 4,225 часов записей 6,823 видов животных с аннотациями 22 экологических признаков. Система выравнивает аудио и текстовые представления с помощью таксономических структур, что позволяет лучше распознавать виды, не встречавшиеся во время обучения. AnimalCLAP превосходит стандартный CLAP благодаря эффективному использованию экологических и биологических атрибутов, извлекаемых прямо из звуковых записей.

en.title: Enhancing Species Classification with Taxonomy-Aware Audio Analysis
en.desc: AnimalCLAP is a novel framework that enhances species classification from animal vocalizations by integrating hierarchical biological information. It utilizes a large dataset of 4,225 hours of audio recordings from 6,823 species, annotated with ecological traits, to improve model training. The framework aligns audio and textual data through taxonomic structures, allowing it to better recognize species that were not included in the training set. This approach leads to improved performance in identifying unseen species compared to previous models like CLAP.

zh.title: 利用分类法提升动物声音识别的能力
zh.desc: AnimalCLAP是一个基于分类法的语言-音频框架，利用层次生物信息来提高动物声音的物种分类。该框架通过结合生态特征和分类结构，显著提升了对未见物种的识别能力。我们构建了一个包含4225小时录音和6823个物种的新数据集，并标注了22种生态特征。AnimalCLAP模型在此数据集上训练，能够更好地对齐音频和文本表示，从而改善对物种的识别效果。

papers.title: Perceptio: Perception Enhanced Vision Language Models via Spatial Token Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Perceptio enhances vision-language models with explicit spatial reasoning through integrated semantic segmentation and depth tokens generated via VQ-VAE distillation and multi-task learning.  AI-generated summary Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) excel at semantic understanding but struggle with fine grained spatial grounding, as the model must implicitly infer complex geometry without ever producing a spatial interpretation. We present Perceptio, a perception enhanced LVLM with 2D and 3D spatial reasoning abilities, enabled via explicit semantic segmentation tokens and depth tokens generated directly within the autoregressive sequence. Concretely, we (i) distill a VQVAE depth codebook from a strong monocular teacher to tokenize dense depth into compact sequences, and (ii) integrate SAM2 based semantic segmentation tokens and VQ-VAE depth tokens inside the LLM so the model first emits spatial tokens and then answers. To stabilize depth token generation, we introduce novel composite depth-token objectives (marker, token, and count losses) and a soft-merging technique for differentiable reconstruction. We adopt a multi-task co-training strategy across diverse datasets, letting the model learn perception tokens to tackle multiple downstream tasks. Building on InternVL, Perceptio achieves state-of-the-art performance across benchmarks: improving referring expression segmentation by +0.8/+1.4/+1.1 cIoU on RefCOCO/+/g HardBLINK spatial understanding accuracy by 10.3%, and MMBench accuracy by 1.0%, demonstrating that explicit spatial chain-of-thought materially strengthens spatial grounding in LVLMs.

ru.title: Явное пространственное мышление в видение-языковых моделях
ru.desc: Perceptio улучшает видение-языковые модели путём добавления явного пространственного рассуждения через интеграцию токенов семантической сегментации и глубины. Авторы используют дистилляцию VQVAE для создания компактного кодека глубины и встраивают токены, основанные на SAM2, непосредственно в авторегрессивную последовательность LLM. Для стабилизации генерации токенов глубины предложены композитные loss-функции и техника мягкого слияния для дифференцируемой реконструкции. Модель обучается на нескольких задачах одновременно, что позволяет достичь улучшений в семантической сегментации, пространственном понимании и общей точности.

en.title: Enhancing Spatial Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
en.desc: Perceptio is a new model that improves vision-language understanding by adding explicit spatial reasoning capabilities. It uses semantic segmentation and depth tokens, which are created through a process called VQ-VAE distillation, to help the model understand both 2D and 3D spaces better. By integrating these tokens into the model's output sequence, Perceptio can generate spatial information before answering questions, enhancing its accuracy. The model has shown significant improvements in various benchmarks, demonstrating that clear spatial reasoning is crucial for better performance in language and vision tasks.

zh.title: 增强空间推理的视觉语言模型
zh.desc: Perceptio 是一种增强视觉语言模型的技术，通过集成语义分割和深度标记来实现明确的空间推理。该模型解决了大规模视觉语言模型在空间定位方面的不足，使其能够处理复杂的几何关系。具体来说，Perceptio 通过从强大的单目教师模型中提取 VQVAE 深度代码本，将密集深度信息转化为紧凑的序列，并将语义分割标记与深度标记结合在一起。通过多任务共同训练，Perceptio 在多个基准测试中取得了最先进的性能，显著提高了空间理解的准确性。

papers.title: Effective Strategies for Asynchronous Software Engineering Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Multi-agent collaboration for software engineering tasks faces challenges in coordination and synchronization, which are addressed through a structured paradigm using centralized delegation, asynchronous execution, and isolated workspaces.  AI-generated summary AI agents have become increasingly capable at isolated software engineering (SWE) tasks such as resolving issues on Github. Yet long-horizon tasks involving multiple interdependent subtasks still pose challenges both with respect to accuracy, and with respect to timely completion. A natural approach to solving these long-horizon tasks in a timely manner is asynchronous multi-agent collaboration, where multiple agents work on different parts of the task at the same time. But effective application of multi-agent systems has proven surprisingly difficult: concurrent edits by multiple agents interfere with each other, dependencies are difficult to synchronize, and combining partial progress into a coherent whole is challenging. On the other hand, human developers have long relied on mature collaboration infrastructure to manage these challenges in large software projects. Inspired by these collaboration primitives, we introduce Centralized Asynchronous Isolated Delegation (CAID), a structured multi-agent coordination paradigm grounded in three core SWE primitives: centralized task delegation, asynchronous execution, and isolated workspaces. CAID constructs dependency-aware task plans through a central manager, executes subtasks concurrently in isolated workspaces, and consolidates progress via structured integration with executable test-based verification. In empirical evaluation, we find that CAID improves accuracy over single-agent baselines by 26.7% absolute on paper reproduction tasks (PaperBench) and 14.3% on Python library development tasks (Commit0). Through systematic analysis, we find that branch-and-merge is a central coordination mechanism for multi-agent collaboration, and that SWE primitives such as git worktree, git commit, and git merge enable it to be realized in a reliable and executable manner.
affiliations: Carnegie Mellon University, Language Technologies Institute

ru.title: Координированная асинхронная работа AI-агентов в разработке ПО через изолированные рабочие пространства
ru.desc: В работе предложен подход CAID (Centralized Asynchronous Isolated Delegation) для координации нескольких AI-агентов при решении сложных задач разработки программного обеспечения. Система использует централизованного менеджера для составления плана задач с учетом зависимостей, позволяет агентам работать асинхронно в изолированных рабочих пространствах и объединяет результаты через механизм ветвления и слияния кода. Экспериментальные результаты показывают улучшение точности на 26.7% для задач воспроизведения статей и на 14.3% для разработки Python-библиотек по сравнению с одноагентными подходами. Ключевым механизмом координации выступает операция branch-and-merge, реализованная через стандартные инструменты контроля версий.

en.title: Enhancing Multi-Agent Collaboration in Software Engineering with CAID
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach called Centralized Asynchronous Isolated Delegation (CAID) to improve multi-agent collaboration in software engineering tasks. CAID addresses issues like coordination and synchronization by using a central manager to delegate tasks, allowing agents to work asynchronously in isolated environments. This method helps manage dependencies and integrates partial work effectively, leading to better accuracy in completing complex tasks. Empirical results show that CAID significantly outperforms single-agent methods in both paper reproduction and Python library development tasks.

zh.title: 集中式异步隔离委托：提升多智能体协作的效率
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了多智能体在软件工程任务中的协作问题，提出了一种名为集中式异步隔离委托（CAID）的新方法。CAID通过集中任务委托、异步执行和隔离工作空间来解决多智能体协作中的协调和同步挑战。研究表明，CAID在纸质重现任务和Python库开发任务中，准确性分别提高了26.7%和14.3%。此外，论文还分析了分支与合并作为多智能体协作的核心协调机制，并指出了git等工具在实现这一机制中的重要性。

papers.title: Agentic AI and the next intelligence explosion
papers.abstract: Abstract  The "AI singularity" is often miscast as a monolithic, godlike mind. Evolution suggests a different path: intelligence is fundamentally plural, social, and relational. Recent advances in agentic AI reveal that frontier reasoning models, such as DeepSeek-R1, do not improve simply by "thinking longer". Instead, they simulate internal "societies of thought," spontaneous cognitive debates that argue, verify, and reconcile to solve complex tasks. Moreover, we are entering an era of human-AI centaurs: hybrid actors where collective agency transcends individual control. Scaling this intelligence requires shifting from dyadic alignment (RLHF) toward institutional alignment. By designing digital protocols, modeled on organizations and markets, we can build a social infrastructure of checks and balances. The next intelligence explosion will not be a single silicon brain, but a complex, combinatorial society specializing and sprawling like a city. No mind is an island.

ru.title: Интеллект как общество: от одного ума к коллективному разуму
ru.desc: В статье утверждается, что искусственный интеллект развивается не как монолитное сознание, а как множество взаимодействующих агентов, образующих общество мыслей. Авторы показывают, что продвинутые рассуждающие модели, такие как DeepSeek-R1, решают сложные задачи через внутренние дебаты и верификацию, а не просто увеличением времени обработки. Они предлагают переход от парной адаптации через RLHF к институциональной адаптации через цифровые протоколы, похожие на организационные структуры. Масштабирование интеллекта требует проектирования социальной инфраструктуры с системой сдержек и противовесов, как в городе или рыночной экосистеме.

en.title: Intelligence as a Collaborative Society, Not a Solo Mind
en.desc: This paper discusses the concept of the 'AI singularity' and argues that intelligence should be viewed as diverse and collaborative rather than as a single, powerful entity. It highlights how advanced AI models, like DeepSeek-R1, utilize internal debates among different cognitive processes to tackle complex problems effectively. The authors propose that future AI systems will function as hybrid entities, combining human and AI capabilities, which necessitates a shift from simple reinforcement learning methods to more complex institutional alignment strategies. By creating digital protocols that mimic social structures, we can foster a balanced and cooperative AI ecosystem, leading to a new era of intelligence that resembles a thriving city rather than a solitary mind.

zh.title: 智能的未来：多元与社会化的AI
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了人工智能的未来，强调智能是多元的、社会性的和关系性的。最新的代理AI模型，如DeepSeek-R1，并不是通过延长思考时间来提高性能，而是通过模拟内部的“思想社会”进行认知辩论。我们正进入一个人类与AI的混合时代，集体智能超越了个体控制。为了实现这种智能的扩展，需要从二元对齐转向制度对齐，通过设计数字协议来建立社会基础设施。

papers.title: Understanding Behavior Cloning with Action Quantization
papers.abstract: Abstract Behavior cloning with quantized actions in autoregressive models achieves optimal sample complexity under stability and smoothness conditions, with quantization error affecting horizon-dependent performance.  AI-generated summary Behavior cloning is a fundamental paradigm in machine learning, enabling policy learning from expert demonstrations across robotics, autonomous driving, and generative models. Autoregressive models like transformer have proven remarkably effective, from large language models (LLMs) to vision-language-action systems (VLAs). However, applying autoregressive models to continuous control requires discretizing actions through quantization, a practice widely adopted yet poorly understood theoretically. This paper provides theoretical foundations for this practice. We analyze how quantization error propagates along the horizon and interacts with statistical sample complexity. We show that behavior cloning with quantized actions and log-loss achieves optimal sample complexity, matching existing lower bounds, and incurs only polynomial horizon dependence on quantization error, provided the dynamics are stable and the policy satisfies a probabilistic smoothness condition. We further characterize when different quantization schemes satisfy or violate these requirements, and propose a model-based augmentation that provably improves the error bound without requiring policy smoothness. Finally, we establish fundamental limits that jointly capture the effects of quantization error and statistical complexity.

ru.title: Теория квантования действий в автарегрессивном поведенческом клонировании
ru.desc: В статье исследуется поведенческое клонирование с квантованными действиями в автарегрессивных моделях, которые достигают оптимальной выборочной сложности при условиях стабильности и гладкости политики. Авторы анализируют, как ошибка квантования распространяется вдоль горизонта предсказания и взаимодействует со статистической сложностью обучения. Доказывается, что такой подход обеспечивает оптимальную сложность по выборке и полиномиальную зависимость ошибки от горизонта при соблюдении условий на динамику среды. Предложена модельная аугментация, которая улучшает границы ошибки без требования гладкости политики.

en.title: Optimizing Behavior Cloning with Quantized Actions
en.desc: This paper explores behavior cloning in machine learning, focusing on how quantized actions in autoregressive models can optimize sample efficiency. It demonstrates that quantization error impacts performance based on the prediction horizon and the stability of the system. The authors provide theoretical insights into how quantization interacts with statistical sample complexity, showing that optimal sample complexity can be achieved under certain conditions. Additionally, they propose a model-based approach to enhance performance without needing smoothness in the policy, while also identifying the limits of quantization error and statistical complexity.

zh.title: 量化动作下的行为克隆优化样本复杂度
zh.desc: 本文探讨了在自回归模型中使用量化动作进行行为克隆的理论基础。行为克隆是一种机器学习方法，通过专家示范学习策略，广泛应用于机器人、自动驾驶等领域。研究表明，在稳定性和光滑性条件下，使用量化动作的行为克隆可以实现最佳样本复杂度，并且量化误差对性能的影响是多项式级别的。本文还提出了一种基于模型的增强方法，可以在不要求策略光滑性的情况下改善误差界限。

papers.title: ToolRosetta: Bridging Open-Source Repositories and Large Language Model Agents through Automated Tool Standardization
papers.abstract: Abstract  Reusing and invoking existing code remains costly and unreliable, as most practical tools are embedded in heterogeneous code repositories and lack standardized, executable interfaces. Although large language models (LLMs) and Model Context Protocol (MCP)-based tool invocation frameworks enable natural language task execution, current approaches rely heavily on manual tool curation and standardization, which fundamentally limits scalability. In this paper, we propose ToolRosetta, a unified framework that automatically translates open-source code repositories and APIs into MCP-compatible tools that can be reliably invoked by LLMs. Given a user task, ToolRosetta autonomously plans toolchains, identifies relevant codebases, and converts them into executable MCP services, enabling end-to-end task completion with minimal human intervention. In addition, ToolRosetta incorporates a security inspection layer to mitigate risks inherent in executing arbitrary code. Extensive experiments across diverse scientific domains demonstrate that ToolRosetta can automatically standardize a large number of open-source tools and reduce the human effort required for code reproduction and deployment. Notably, by seamlessly leveraging specialized open-source tools, ToolRosetta-powered agents consistently improve task completion performance compared to commercial LLMs and existing agent systems.

ru.title: Автоматическая стандартизация кода для интеграции с языковыми моделями
ru.desc: В статье предлагается ToolRosetta — фреймворк, который автоматически преобразует открытые репозитории кода и API в инструменты, совместимые с Model Context Protocol, чтобы большие языковые модели могли их надёжно использовать. Система самостоятельно планирует цепочки инструментов, находит нужные кодовые базы и конвертирует их в исполняемые MCP-сервисы, минимизируя человеческое вмешательство. Фреймворк включает слой безопасности для защиты от рисков выполнения произвольного кода. Эксперименты показывают, что ToolRosetta значительно снижает затраты на стандартизацию инструментов и воспроизведение кода, помогая агентам на основе LLM превосходить коммерческие модели.

en.title: Automating Code Reuse with ToolRosetta
en.desc: This paper introduces ToolRosetta, a framework designed to enhance the usability of open-source code by converting it into tools that can be easily invoked by large language models (LLMs). It addresses the challenges of manual tool curation and standardization, which limit the scalability of existing solutions. ToolRosetta autonomously identifies relevant codebases, plans toolchains, and transforms them into executable services compatible with the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Additionally, it includes a security inspection layer to ensure safe execution of code, significantly reducing the human effort needed for code deployment and improving task completion performance in various scientific domains.

zh.title: ToolRosetta：自动化工具调用的新框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了ToolRosetta，一个统一框架，能够自动将开源代码库和API转换为与模型上下文协议（MCP）兼容的工具，从而使大型语言模型（LLMs）能够可靠地调用这些工具。ToolRosetta能够根据用户任务自主规划工具链，识别相关代码库，并将其转换为可执行的MCP服务，极大地减少了人工干预。该框架还包含安全检查层，以降低执行任意代码时的风险。实验结果表明，ToolRosetta能够自动标准化大量开源工具，并显著提高任务完成性能。
